behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a bedroll item. When the item is examined ('U8P_OPNBDROL::look' event) it displays the label "bedroll ".

When the item is used ('use' event) the script collects the item's world coordinates (X, Y, Z) and delegates to the engine's FREE subsystem (FREE::2411) with those coordinates, the item pointer, and an identifier (0x0216) to initiate the bedroll's runtime action (likely open/activate/sleep).
No global flags are read or written by these handlers; behavior is localized to displaying text on U8P_OPNBDROL::look and delegating a use-action to the runtime on use.
notable_control_flow: U8P_OPNBDROL::OPNBDROL::look: simple linear flow — prepare context, call Item::bark with the static string "bedroll ", free temporary string, then suspend/return.

U8P_OPNBDROL::OPNBDROL::use: sequentially calls Item::getZ, Item::getY, Item::getX to collect coordinates, then calls FREE::2411 with (X, Y, Z, 0, 0x0216, Item* , extra/process info) to hand control to engine-level behavior.
The 'U8P_OPNBDROL::look' and 'use' behaviors are separate entry events (Event 0 and Event 1 respectively) rather than one calling the other.
open_questions: What exact runtime behavior or script is triggered by FREE::2411 when passed identifier 0x0216 (inferred to be bedroll-use behavior but the concrete effect is not present in this excerpt)?

What is the meaning and origin of the pushed 'pid' value observed in the script (appears to be process/player context used when setting process info/exclusion) and how it impacts downstream processing?
Are there any additional side-effects or state changes performed by the FREE::2411 handler (e.g., starting a sleep timer, playing an animation, spawning a UI) that are not visible here?

main_function.name: U8P_OPNBDROL::look
main_function.purpose: Present the item's textual label/description when the bedroll is examined by a player/process (bark the string "bedroll ").
entry_conditions: Event 0: triggered when the actor looks at/examines the bedroll item (examine/inspect interaction).

function_map.name: U8P_OPNBDROL::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_OPNBDROL::look' (examine) event for the bedroll; display the item's label via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_OPNBDROL::use
function_map.role: Handle the 'use' event for the bedroll; collect coordinates and invoke FREE subsystem to perform the use action.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item-level call to display/emit a short text message from an item/process to a player or process.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return the item's Z coordinate for use by runtime handlers.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return the item's Y coordinate for use by runtime handlers.

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return the item's X coordinate for use by runtime handlers.

function_map.name: FREE::2411
function_map.role: Engine/free subsystem entry invoked to start the bedroll-use behavior at specified coordinates with the given item and identifier (0x0216).

external_calls.purpose: Display the static label "bedroll " to the inspecting process/player.
Retrieve the bedroll's Z (height/level) coordinate to pass to the FREE handler.
Retrieve the bedroll's Y coordinate to pass to the FREE handler.
Retrieve the bedroll's X coordinate to pass to the FREE handler.
Delegate the actual 'use' runtime behavior (identified by id 0x0216) to engine-level code, supplying coordinates and the item pointer.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a bedroll item. When the item is examined ('U8P_OPNBDROL::look' event) it displays the label "bedroll ".
